title: Communitarianism: politics, society and public policy [Book Review]
author: Agbor

opening_move.description: The review opens by positioning Tam's communitarianism as a timely intervention that moves beyond abstract philosophy toward practical applicability — a forward-looking framework built on mutual responsibility, cooperative enquiry, and citizen participation.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Communitarianism needs to be recovered as a practical political framework for addressing contemporary social challenges, not as nostalgic conservatism

theoretical_positioning.description: The review presents Tam's practical communitarianism appreciatively, without independent critique.

epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre. Tam's book itself applies communitarian principles to concrete policy domains; the review is primarily expository.

case_type.case_name: Tam, Communitarianism: Politics, Society and Public Policy (2025)

confidence_notes: Book review genre; largely expository. Classified as normative_with_qualitative_empirical to flag that Tam's book applies theory to policy domains, even though this review doesn't engage evidence directly.
